---
title: "model_fitting"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-28"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse, # Data manipulation and plotting
  dplyr,
  here, # Robust file paths
  ggplot2, #ggplot
  cmdstanr,
  #loo,
  bayesplot, #Plotting MCMC
  furrr,        # Parallel processing
  future        # Future backend
)

# Parallel Setup: Leave 2 cores free for system stability
n_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 2
plan(multisession, workers = n_cores)

# Optional: Set a default ggplot theme
theme_set(theme_classic())
```

#now we fit the 2 models (WBA and SBA) and fit them to each dataset & then compare which model "best explains" which dataset (3 datasets: selfish, balanced, social)

#not doing expansive param recovery etc. due to time constraints (just the bare minimum)

```{r}
make_stan_data <- function(df) {
  list(
    n                 = nrow(df),
    trust1            = as.integer(df$FirstRating - 1L),
    trust2            = as.integer(df$SecondRating - 1L),
    group_trust_mean  = as.integer(round(df$GroupTrustMean)),
    n_total_rating    = 7L,
    prior_kappa_mu    = 2.0,
    prior_kappa_sigma = 1.0
    #not doing rho and kappa directly now?
  )
}

datasets <- list(
  self_focused        = read_csv("data/self_focused_WBA_agent.csv"),
  balanced            = read_csv("data/SBA_agent.csv"),
  socially_influenced = read_csv("data/socially_influenced_WBA_agent.csv")
)

sampler_args <- list(
  chains          = 4,
  parallel_chains = 4,
  iter_warmup     = 1000,
  iter_sampling   = 1000,
  refresh         = 0
)

sba_model <- cmdstan_model("models/SBA_model2.stan")
wba_model <- cmdstan_model("models/WBA_model2.stan")

results <- map(datasets, function(df) {
  d <- make_stan_data(df)
  list(
    sba = sba_model$sample(
      data            = d,
      fixed_param     = TRUE,
      chains          = 1,
      iter_warmup     = 0,     # fixed params need less?
      iter_sampling   = 500,
      refresh         = 0
    ),
    wba = wba_model$sample(
      data            = d,
      adapt_delta     = 0.95,
      chains          = 4,
      parallel_chains = 4,
      iter_warmup     = 1000,
      iter_sampling   = 1000,
      refresh         = 0
    )
  )
})


# LOO comparison per dataset
loo_results <- map(results, function(r) {
  loo_compare(
    r$sba$loo(),
    r$wba$loo()
  )
})
print(loo_results)
```
```{r}
#quick so can get this out on time (no indepth param recovery on this, just checking the sampling fit summary)
check_sampling <- function(fit, model_name, dataset_name) {
  cat("\n======", model_name, "on", dataset_name, "======\n")
  
  # 1. Rhat & effective sample size
  print(summary(fit)$summary[, c("mean", "sd", "n_eff", "Rhat")])
  
  # 2. Divergences
  sampler_params <- get_sampler_params(fit, inc_warmup = FALSE)
  divergences <- sum(sapply(sampler_params, function(x) sum(x[, "divergent__"])))
  cat("Divergent transitions:", divergences, "\n")
  
  # 3. Tree depth
  treedepths <- sum(sapply(sampler_params, function(x) sum(x[, "treedepth__"] >= 10)))
  cat("Max treedepth hits:", treedepths, "\n")
  
  # 4. Trace plots (to get visually)
  posterior <- as.array(fit)
  if (dim(posterior)[3] > 1) {  # skip if no freeparams (SBA)
    mcmc_trace(posterior, pars = c("w_direct", "w_social", "rho", "kappa")) +
      ggtitle(paste(model_name, "-", dataset_name))
  }
}

# run on all fits
iwalk(results, function(r, dataset_name) {
  check_sampling(r$sba, "SBA", dataset_name)
  check_sampling(r$wba, "WBA", dataset_name)
})
```
